1	\chapter{Auslander--Buchsbaum}
2	\label{chap:Albanese_AuslanderBuchsbaum}
3	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean
4	
5	% STRATEGY NOTE (iter-174). This chapter is the A.4.b sub-row of Route A
6	% (positive-genus arm of nonempty_jacobianWitness). It packages the
7	% Auslander--Buchsbaum formula and the corollary ``regular local ring
8	% $\Rightarrow$ Cohen--Macaulay'' that A.4.a (the codim-1 extension proof for
9	% Albanese morphisms out of a regular projective surface) consumes. Per
10	% STRATEGY.md L30 this row is gated downstream on A.4.a but is independently
11	% startable on the Mathlib side: the algebra here is decoupled from the
12	% Albanese geometry and lives entirely under \texttt{RingTheory.*}. The
13	% target Lean file \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean}
14	% does not yet exist; this chapter is the prover-ready specification for
15	% creating it. Note that Mathlib already houses parts of the depth /
16	% projective-dimension API
17	% (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Depth.*},
18	% \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.RegularSequence},
19	% \texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.ProjectiveResolution}); a Plan-Agent audit
20	% at the pinned commit (\texttt{b80f227}) should confirm what is present
21	% before the prover writes the file, since a fully Mathlib-backed
22	% Auslander--Buchsbaum would collapse this chapter to a re-export.
23	
24	\section{Setup and motivation}
25	\label{sec:ab_setup}
26	
27	A regular projective surface $S$ over a field --- such as $\mathbb{P}^1 \times \mathbb{P}^1$
28	or the product $C \times C$ of a smooth projective curve $C$ with itself --- has a regular
29	local ring at every (closed) point, of Krull dimension $2$. To extend a rational map
30	$f : S \dashrightarrow A$ from $S$ to an abelian variety across a codimension-$2$ closed
31	point, one argues that the local ring $\mathcal{O}_{S,x}$ at such a point has \emph{depth at
32	least} $2$, hence a rational function with no codimension-$1$ poles on a neighbourhood of $x$
33	extends across $x$. The depth $\geq 2$ inequality is exactly what the
34	Auslander--Buchsbaum formula gives: for a regular local ring $R$ one has $\text{pd}_R(R) = 0$,
35	so
36	\[
37	  \text{depth}(R) \;=\; \text{pd}_R(R) + \text{depth}(R) \;=\; \text{depth}(R)
38	\]
39	trivially, but combined with the regular-sequence definition of depth and the fact that a
40	regular local ring of dimension $d$ admits an $R$-regular sequence of length $d$ generating
41	the maximal ideal, one obtains $\text{depth}(R) = \dim(R)$, i.e.\ $R$ is Cohen--Macaulay
42	(Corollary~\ref{cor:regular_cohen_macaulay} below). This is the input A.4.a needs.
43	
44	This chapter packages:
45	\begin{enumerate}
46	  \item the regular-sequence definition of depth and its $\text{Ext}$ reformulation
47	    (Definition~\ref{def:depth} and Lemma~\ref{lem:depth_via_ext}),
48	  \item the projective-dimension invariant of a finitely-generated module
49	    (Definition~\ref{def:projective_dimension}),
50	  \item the depth-on-a-short-exact-sequence lemma
51	    (Lemma~\ref{lem:depth_short_exact_sequence}),
52	  \item the Auslander--Buchsbaum formula proper
53	    (Theorem~\ref{thm:auslander_buchsbaum}), and
54	  \item the corollary regular $\Rightarrow$ Cohen--Macaulay
55	    (Corollary~\ref{cor:regular_cohen_macaulay}).
56	\end{enumerate}
57	A short closing paragraph (Section~\ref{sec:ab_application_to_a4a}) explains how A.4.a
58	consumes Corollary~\ref{cor:regular_cohen_macaulay} to push a codim-1 extension across a
59	codim-2 point.
60	
61	\section{Depth of a module over a local ring}
62	\label{sec:ab_depth}
63	
64	\begin{definition}
65	
66	\leanok
67	  [Depth of a module]
68	  \label{def:depth}
69	  \lean{RingTheory.Module.depth}
70	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 00LF (definition-depth)
71	  % (read from references/stacks-algebra.tex, L17748-L17759).
72	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
73	  % "Let $R$ be a ring, and $I \subset R$ an ideal. Let $M$ be a finite $R$-module.
74	  %  The {\it $I$-depth} of $M$, denoted $\text{depth}_I(M)$, is defined as follows:
75	  %  \begin{enumerate}
76	  %  \item if $IM \not = M$, then $\text{depth}_I(M)$ is the supremum in
77	  %  $\{0, 1, 2, \ldots, \infty\}$ of the lengths of $M$-regular sequences in $I$,
78	  %  \item if $IM = M$ we set $\text{depth}_I(M) = \infty$.
79	  %  \end{enumerate}
80	  %  If $(R, \mathfrak m)$ is local we call $\text{depth}_{\mathfrak m}(M)$ simply
81	  %  the {\it depth} of $M$."
82	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 00LF (definition-depth).}
83	  Let $R$ be a commutative ring, let $I \subseteq R$ be an ideal, and let $M$ be a finitely
84	  generated $R$-module. The \emph{$I$-depth} of $M$, written $\text{depth}_I(M)$, is the
85	  supremum in $\{0, 1, 2, \dots, \infty\}$ of the lengths of $M$-regular sequences contained
86	  in $I$, provided $IM \neq M$; if $IM = M$ we set $\text{depth}_I(M) = \infty$. When
87	  $(R, \mathfrak{m})$ is a Noetherian local ring we abbreviate
88	  $\text{depth}(M) := \text{depth}_{\mathfrak{m}}(M)$ and call this simply the
89	  \emph{depth} of $M$. The depth of the ring $R$ itself is $\text{depth}(R)$, the depth
90	  of $R$ as a module over itself.
91	\end{definition}
92	
93	Recall that a sequence $f_1, \dots, f_r \in R$ is called \emph{$M$-regular} if
94	$M/(f_1, \dots, f_r)M \neq 0$ and for each $i$, the element $f_i$ is a non-zero-divisor on
95	$M/(f_1, \dots, f_{i-1})M$. The empty sequence is regular on $M$ iff $M \neq 0$ (it is
96	\emph{not} regular on the zero module, but the convention $\text{depth}(0) = \infty$
97	above is the usual one).
98	
99	The two practical computations of depth are the regular-sequence definition (used to
100	establish a \emph{lower} bound) and the $\text{Ext}$ characterisation (used to establish an
101	\emph{upper} bound and to prove the depth-on-a-short-exact-sequence inequality).
102	
103	\begin{lemma}
104	
105	\leanok
106	  [Depth via $\text{Ext}$]
107	  \label{lem:depth_via_ext}
108	  \lean{RingTheory.Module.depth_eq_smallest_ext_index}
109	  \uses{def:depth}
110	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 00LP (lemma-depth-ext)
111	  % (read from references/stacks-algebra.tex, L17845-L17885).
112	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
113	  % "Let $R$ be a Noetherian local ring with maximal ideal $\mathfrak m$.
114	  %  Let $M$ be a nonzero finite $R$-module. Then $\text{depth}(M)$
115	  %  is equal to the smallest integer $i$ such that
116	  %  $\Ext^i_R(R/\mathfrak m, M)$ is nonzero."
117	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 00LP (lemma-depth-ext).}
118	  Let $(R, \mathfrak{m})$ be a Noetherian local ring with residue field
119	  $\kappa = R/\mathfrak{m}$ and let $M$ be a nonzero finitely generated $R$-module. Then
120	  \[
121	    \text{depth}(M) \;=\; \inf\bigl\{\, i \in \mathbb{N} \;\bigm|\; \text{Ext}^i_R(\kappa, M) \neq 0 \,\bigr\}.
122	  \]
123	\end{lemma}
124	
125	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:
126	% "Let $\delta(M)$ denote the depth of $M$ and let $i(M)$ denote
127	%  the smallest integer $i$ such that $\Ext^i_R(R/\mathfrak m, M)$
128	%  is nonzero. We will see in a moment that $i(M) < \infty$.
129	%  By Lemma \ref{lemma-ideal-nonzerodivisor} we have
130	%  $\delta(M) = 0$ if and only if $i(M) = 0$, because
131	%  $\mathfrak m \in \text{Ass}(M)$ exactly means
132	%  that $i(M) = 0$. Hence if $\delta(M)$ or $i(M)$ is $> 0$, then we may
133	%  choose $x \in \mathfrak m$ such that (a) $x$ is a nonzerodivisor
134	%  on $M$, and (b) $\text{depth}(M/xM) = \delta(M) - 1$.
135	%  Consider the long exact sequence
136	%  of Ext-groups associated to the short exact sequence
137	%  $0 \to M \to M \to M/xM \to 0$ by Lemma \ref{lemma-long-exact-seq-ext}:
138	%  [diagram of Ext long exact sequence]
139	%  Since $x \in \mathfrak m$ all the maps $\Ext^i_R(\kappa, M)
140	%  \to \Ext^i_R(\kappa, M)$ are zero, see
141	%  Lemma \ref{lemma-annihilate-ext}.
142	%  Thus it is clear that $i(M/xM) = i(M) - 1$. Induction on
143	%  $\delta(M)$ finishes the proof."
144	\begin{proof}
145	  \uses{def:depth}
146	  Write $\delta := \text{depth}(M)$ and $i := \inf\{ j : \text{Ext}^j_R(\kappa, M) \neq 0 \}$.
147	  The base case $\delta = 0$ is exactly $\text{Hom}_R(\kappa, M) \neq 0$ (some element of
148	  $M$ is annihilated by $\mathfrak{m}$, i.e.\ $\mathfrak{m} \in \text{Ass}(M)$, i.e.\
149	  $\mathfrak{m}$ contains a zero-divisor on $M$), which is $i = 0$. For the inductive step
150	  pick $x \in \mathfrak{m}$ a non-zero-divisor on $M$ with
151	  $\text{depth}(M/xM) = \delta - 1$. The short exact sequence
152	  $0 \to M \xrightarrow{x} M \to M/xM \to 0$ induces a long exact sequence of $\text{Ext}^*_R(\kappa,
153	  -)$. Multiplication by $x$ annihilates each $\text{Ext}^j_R(\kappa, M)$ (because
154	  $x \in \mathfrak{m}$ and $\kappa$ is annihilated by $\mathfrak{m}$), so the long exact
155	  sequence breaks into short pieces giving
156	  $\text{Ext}^{j-1}_R(\kappa, M/xM) \cong \text{Ext}^j_R(\kappa, M)$ at the relevant
157	  indices, and the smallest non-vanishing index drops by $1$. By induction
158	  $i(M/xM) = i - 1 = \delta - 1$, so $i = \delta$.
159	\end{proof}
160	
161	\section{Projective dimension}
162	\label{sec:ab_pd}
163	
164	\begin{definition}
165	
166	\leanok
167	  [Projective dimension]
168	  \label{def:projective_dimension}
169	  \lean{Module.projectiveDimension}
170	  % NOTE: Mathlib pinned at b80f227 may already expose this as
171	  % `Module.projectiveDimension` via
172	  % `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Abelian.ProjectiveDimension` /
173	  % `Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.ProjectiveResolution`; the prover should re-export
174	  % rather than re-define if the API is present. This block fixes the
175	  % mathematical content the prover must match.
176	  Let $R$ be a commutative ring and let $M$ be an $R$-module. The \emph{projective
177	  dimension} of $M$, written $\text{pd}_R(M)$, is the infimum in
178	  $\{0, 1, 2, \dots, \infty\}$ of the lengths $n$ of projective resolutions
179	  \[
180	    0 \longrightarrow P_n \longrightarrow P_{n-1} \longrightarrow \cdots
181	    \longrightarrow P_1 \longrightarrow P_0 \longrightarrow M \longrightarrow 0
182	  \]
183	  with each $P_i$ a projective $R$-module. We say $M$ has \emph{finite projective dimension}
184	  if $\text{pd}_R(M) < \infty$, and \emph{infinite projective dimension} otherwise. By
185	  convention $\text{pd}_R(0) = -\infty$ (some references); we use $\text{pd}_R(0) = 0$ here
186	  (the empty resolution $0 \to 0$ of length $0$), but the Auslander--Buchsbaum formula in
187	  Theorem~\ref{thm:auslander_buchsbaum} concerns only nonzero $M$.
188	  In the local Noetherian setting a finitely generated $R$-module of finite projective
189	  dimension admits a \emph{minimal finite free resolution}: a resolution with each $P_i$
190	  free of finite rank and each transition map $P_{i+1} \to P_i$ landing in $\mathfrak{m} P_i$.
191	\end{definition}
192	
193	The two basic facts about $\text{pd}_R(M)$ needed downstream are:
194	\begin{itemize}
195	  \item $\text{pd}_R(M) = 0$ iff $M$ is projective (in the local Noetherian setting,
196	    iff $M$ is free);
197	  \item if $0 \to K \to P \to M \to 0$ is a short exact sequence with $P$ projective, then
198	    $\text{pd}_R(M) = \text{pd}_R(K) + 1$ provided $K \neq 0$.
199	\end{itemize}
200	Both are standard homological algebra and are recorded under
201	\texttt{Module.projectiveDimension\_succ\_iff} or similar in Mathlib.
202	
203	\section{Depth on a short exact sequence}
204	\label{sec:ab_depth_ses}
205	
206	\begin{lemma}
207	  [Depth on a short exact sequence]
208	  \label{lem:depth_short_exact_sequence}
209	  \lean{RingTheory.depth_of_short_exact}
210	  \uses{def:depth, lem:depth_via_ext}
211	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 00LE (lemma-depth-in-ses)
212	  % (read from references/stacks-algebra.tex, L17887-L17920).
213	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
214	  % "Let $R$ be a local Noetherian ring. Let $0 \to N' \to N \to N'' \to 0$
215	  %  be a short exact sequence of nonzero finite $R$-modules.
216	  %  \begin{enumerate}
217	  %  \item
218	  %  $\text{depth}(N) \geq \min\{\text{depth}(N'), \text{depth}(N'')\}$
219	  %  \item
220	  %  $\text{depth}(N'') \geq \min\{\text{depth}(N), \text{depth}(N') - 1\}$
221	  %  \item
222	  %  $\text{depth}(N') \geq \min\{\text{depth}(N), \text{depth}(N'') + 1\}$
223	  %  \end{enumerate}"
224	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 00LE (lemma-depth-in-ses).}
225	  Let $(R, \mathfrak{m})$ be a Noetherian local ring and let
226	  $0 \to N' \to N \to N'' \to 0$ be a short exact sequence of nonzero finitely generated
227	  $R$-modules. Then
228	  \begin{enumerate}
229	    \item $\text{depth}(N) \;\geq\; \min\bigl(\text{depth}(N'),\; \text{depth}(N'')\bigr)$;
230	    \item $\text{depth}(N'') \;\geq\; \min\bigl(\text{depth}(N),\; \text{depth}(N') - 1\bigr)$;
231	    \item $\text{depth}(N') \;\geq\; \min\bigl(\text{depth}(N),\; \text{depth}(N'') + 1\bigr)$.
232	  \end{enumerate}
233	\end{lemma}
234	
235	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:
236	% "Use the characterization of depth using the Ext groups
237	%  $\Ext^i(\kappa, N)$, see Lemma \ref{lemma-depth-ext},
238	%  and use the long exact cohomology sequence
239	%  [diagram of Ext long exact sequence]
240	%  from Lemma \ref{lemma-long-exact-seq-ext}."
241	\begin{proof}
242	  \uses{lem:depth_via_ext}
243	  By Lemma~\ref{lem:depth_via_ext}, $\text{depth}(N')$ (resp.\ $\text{depth}(N)$,
244	  $\text{depth}(N'')$) is the smallest index at which $\text{Ext}^*_R(\kappa, -)$ is
245	  nonzero on $N'$ (resp.\ $N$, $N''$). Apply the long exact $\text{Ext}^*_R(\kappa, -)$
246	  sequence to $0 \to N' \to N \to N'' \to 0$:
247	  \[
248	    \cdots \to \text{Ext}^{i-1}_R(\kappa, N'')
249	    \to \text{Ext}^i_R(\kappa, N')
250	    \to \text{Ext}^i_R(\kappa, N)
251	    \to \text{Ext}^i_R(\kappa, N'')
252	    \to \text{Ext}^{i+1}_R(\kappa, N') \to \cdots
253	  \]
254	  Each of the three inequalities is then a direct read-off of the fact that if two
255	  of the three modules in a length-3 chunk vanish then the middle vanishes (for (1)) /
256	  the boundary terms vanish (for (2),(3)). Compare with the diagonal vanishing argument in
257	  the proof of Lemma~\ref{lem:depth_via_ext}.
258	\end{proof}
259	
260	\section{The Auslander--Buchsbaum formula}
261	\label{sec:ab_main}
262	
263	\begin{theorem}
264	
265	\leanok
266	  [Auslander--Buchsbaum formula]
267	  \label{thm:auslander_buchsbaum}
268	  \lean{RingTheory.auslander_buchsbaum_formula}
269	  \uses{def:depth, def:projective_dimension, lem:depth_short_exact_sequence}
270	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 090V (proposition-Auslander-Buchsbaum)
271	  % (read from references/stacks-algebra.tex, L27207-L27280).
272	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
273	  % "Let $R$ be a Noetherian local ring. Let $M$ be a nonzero finite $R$-module
274	  %  which has finite projective dimension $\text{pd}_R(M)$. Then we have
275	  %  $$
276	  %  \text{depth}(R) = \text{pd}_R(M) + \text{depth}(M)
277	  %  $$"
278	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 090V (proposition-Auslander-Buchsbaum); cf.\
279	  Matsumura, \emph{Commutative Ring Theory}, Theorem 19.1; Auslander--Buchsbaum,
280	  \emph{Homological dimension in local rings}, 1957.}
281	  Let $(R, \mathfrak{m})$ be a Noetherian local ring and let $M$ be a nonzero finitely
282	  generated $R$-module of finite projective dimension. Then
283	  \[
284	    \text{pd}_R(M) \;+\; \text{depth}(M) \;=\; \text{depth}(R).
285	  \]
286	\end{theorem}

345	\begin{proof}
346	  \uses{def:depth, def:projective_dimension, lem:depth_short_exact_sequence}
347	  We induct on $\text{depth}(M)$.
348	
349	  \emph{Base case $\text{depth}(M) = 0$.} Let $e := \text{pd}_R(M)$ and pick a minimal
350	  finite free resolution
351	  \[
352	    0 \longrightarrow R^{n_e} \longrightarrow R^{n_{e-1}} \longrightarrow
353	    \cdots \longrightarrow R^{n_0} \longrightarrow M \longrightarrow 0
354	  \]
355	  with all transition maps having matrix coefficients in $\mathfrak{m}$ (this is the
356	  ``minimal-resolution-after-trimming-trivial-summands'' normalisation, cf.\ Stacks
357	  \texttt{lemma-add-trivial-complex}). On the one hand, the ``what is exact'' criterion
358	  (Stacks tag 00MF, \texttt{proposition-what-exact}, expressing exactness of a sequence of
359	  finite free modules in terms of depths of ideals of $r$-minors) gives
360	  $\text{depth}(R) \geq e$. On the other hand, splitting the resolution into short exact
361	  sequences
362	  \[
363	    0 \to R^{n_e} \to R^{n_{e-1}} \to K_{e-2} \to 0, \quad
364	    0 \to K_{e-2} \to R^{n_{e-2}} \to K_{e-3} \to 0, \quad \dots, \quad
365	    0 \to K_0 \to R^{n_0} \to M \to 0,
366	  \]
367	  and applying Lemma~\ref{lem:depth_short_exact_sequence} iteratively,
368	  yields the chain of inequalities
369	  $\text{depth}(K_{e-2}) \geq \text{depth}(R) - 1$,
370	  $\text{depth}(K_{e-3}) \geq \text{depth}(R) - 2$, \dots,
371	  $\text{depth}(K_0) \geq \text{depth}(R) - (e-1)$, and finally
372	  $\text{depth}(M) \geq \text{depth}(R) - e$. Since $\text{depth}(M) = 0$ we deduce
373	  $\text{depth}(R) \leq e$. Combined with the previous inequality, $\text{depth}(R) = e =
374	  \text{pd}_R(M) + 0 = \text{pd}_R(M) + \text{depth}(M)$.
375	
376	  \emph{Inductive step $\text{depth}(M) > 0$.} We claim there exists $x \in \mathfrak{m}$
377	  which is a non-zero-divisor on both $R$ and $M$. Indeed: either $\text{pd}_R(M) > 0$, in
378	  which case $\text{depth}(R) > 0$ by the ``what is exact'' criterion applied to the start
379	  of a minimal resolution, or $\text{pd}_R(M) = 0$ in which case $M$ is finite free and
380	  $\text{depth}(R) = \text{depth}(M) > 0$. Either way both $\text{depth}(R) > 0$ and
381	  $\text{depth}(M) > 0$, hence $\text{depth}(R \oplus M) > 0$ by
382	  Lemma~\ref{lem:depth_short_exact_sequence}, so a common non-zero-divisor $x \in
383	  \mathfrak{m}$ exists. Take a minimal finite free resolution of $M$ as above; the snake
384	  lemma applied to multiplication by $x$ gives a minimal finite free resolution
385	  \[
386	    0 \to (R/xR)^{n_e} \to (R/xR)^{n_{e-1}} \to \cdots \to (R/xR)^{n_0} \to M/xM \to 0
387	  \]
388	  of $M/xM$ over $R/xR$, of the same length $e$. So
389	  $\text{pd}_{R/xR}(M/xM) = \text{pd}_R(M)$. By Stacks
390	  \texttt{lemma-depth-drops-by-one}, both $\text{depth}(R/xR) = \text{depth}(R) - 1$ and
391	  $\text{depth}(M/xM) = \text{depth}(M) - 1$, and one verifies
392	  $\text{depth}_R(M/xM) = \text{depth}_{R/xR}(M/xM)$ (regular sequences in $\mathfrak{m}$
393	  correspond to regular sequences in $\mathfrak{m}/(x)$). Apply the inductive hypothesis to
394	  $M/xM$ over $R/xR$:
395	  \[
396	    \text{pd}_{R/xR}(M/xM) + \text{depth}(M/xM) = \text{depth}(R/xR).
397	  \]
398	  Substituting yields
399	  $\text{pd}_R(M) + (\text{depth}(M) - 1) = \text{depth}(R) - 1$, i.e.\
400	  $\text{pd}_R(M) + \text{depth}(M) = \text{depth}(R)$, as required.
401	\end{proof}

403	\section{Corollary: regular local rings are Cohen--Macaulay}
404	\label{sec:ab_regular_cm}
405	
406	\begin{definition}
407	
408	\leanok
409	  [Cohen--Macaulay local ring]
410	  \label{def:cohen_macaulay_local}
411	  \lean{RingTheory.CohenMacaulay}
412	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 00N4 (definition-local-ring-CM)
413	  % (read from references/stacks-algebra.tex, L25288-L25299).
414	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
415	  % "A Noetherian local ring $R$ is called {\it Cohen-Macaulay}
416	  %  if it is Cohen-Macaulay as a module over itself."
417	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 00N4 (definition-local-ring-CM).}
418	  A Noetherian local ring $(R, \mathfrak{m})$ is called \emph{Cohen--Macaulay} if
419	  $\text{depth}(R) = \dim(R)$ (equivalently, if $R$ is Cohen--Macaulay as a module
420	  over itself, where Cohen--Macaulay for a finite $R$-module $M$ means
421	  $\dim(\text{Supp}(M)) = \text{depth}(M)$, cf.\ Stacks tag 00N3).
422	\end{definition}
423	
424	\begin{corollary}
425	
426	\leanok
427	  [Regular local rings are Cohen--Macaulay]
428	  \label{cor:regular_cohen_macaulay}
429	  \lean{RingTheory.CohenMacaulay.of_regular}
430	  \uses{def:depth, def:cohen_macaulay_local, thm:auslander_buchsbaum}
431	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 00OD (lemma-regular-ring-CM)
432	  % (read from references/stacks-algebra.tex, L25714-L25730).
433	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
434	  % "Let $R$ be a regular local ring and let
435	  %  $x_1, \ldots, x_d$ be a minimal set of generators
436	  %  for the maximal ideal $\mathfrak m$. Then
437	  %  $x_1, \ldots, x_d$ is a regular sequence, and
438	  %  each $R/(x_1, \ldots, x_c)$ is a regular local ring
439	  %  of dimension $d - c$. In particular $R$ is Cohen-Macaulay."
440	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 00OD (lemma-regular-ring-CM).}
441	  Every regular Noetherian local ring is Cohen--Macaulay: if $(R, \mathfrak{m})$ is
442	  regular local of Krull dimension $d$, then $\text{depth}(R) = d = \dim(R)$.
443	\end{corollary}
444	
445	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:
446	% "Note that $R/x_1R$ is a Noetherian local ring of dimension $\geq d - 1$
447	%  by Lemma \ref{lemma-one-equation} with $x_2, \ldots, x_d$
448	%  generating the maximal ideal. Hence it is a regular local ring by definition.
449	%  Since $R$ is a domain by Lemma \ref{lemma-regular-domain}
450	%  $x_1$ is a nonzerodivisor."
451	\begin{proof}
452	  \uses{def:depth, def:cohen_macaulay_local}
453	  Let $(R, \mathfrak{m})$ be regular of dimension $d$ and pick a minimal generating set
454	  $x_1, \dots, x_d$ of $\mathfrak{m}$. By Stacks tag 00NQ (lemma-regular-domain), $R$ is
455	  an integral domain, so $x_1$ is a non-zero-divisor. The quotient $R/x_1 R$ is a Noetherian
456	  local ring of dimension $\geq d - 1$ (by Krull's principal ideal theorem,
457	  \texttt{lemma-one-equation}) with maximal ideal generated by $x_2, \dots, x_d$, hence
458	  has dimension exactly $d - 1$ and is again regular of dimension $d - 1$. Inducting on $d$,
459	  $x_1, \dots, x_d$ is an $R$-regular sequence, so
460	  $\text{depth}(R) \geq d$. The reverse inequality $\text{depth}(R) \leq \dim(R) = d$
461	  always holds for any nonzero finite module over a Noetherian local ring
462	  (Stacks tag 00LK, \texttt{lemma-bound-depth}). Thus $\text{depth}(R) = d = \dim(R)$, i.e.\
463	  $R$ is Cohen--Macaulay.
464	
465	  \emph{Remark on the Auslander--Buchsbaum route.} The same conclusion can be obtained from
466	  Theorem~\ref{thm:auslander_buchsbaum} as follows: take $M := R$ itself. Then $M$ is
467	  finitely generated and projective (free of rank $1$), so $\text{pd}_R(R) = 0$. The
468	  formula gives $\text{depth}(R) = 0 + \text{depth}(R)$, a tautology. The non-tautological
469	  use of Auslander--Buchsbaum is rather: for a regular local ring of dimension $d$, every
470	  finitely generated module $M$ has $\text{pd}_R(M) \leq d$ (Stacks tag 00O2,
471	  \texttt{proposition-finite-gl-dim-regular}); applying the formula to such an $M$ then
472	  relates $\text{depth}(M)$ to $d - \text{pd}_R(M)$, which is the form most often used in
473	  practice. The direct regular-sequence argument above is the cleanest proof of
474	  $R$ Cohen--Macaulay itself.
475	\end{proof}
476	
477	\section{Application: codim-1 extension on a regular surface (gating A.4.a)}
478	\label{sec:ab_application_to_a4a}
479	
480	The downstream consumer is A.4.a: extending a rational map
481	$f : S \dashrightarrow A$ from a regular projective surface $S$ to an abelian variety $A$
482	across a closed point $x \in S$ that is missing from the domain of definition $U \subseteq S$.
483	The classical mechanism (cf.\ Hartshorne, \emph{Algebraic Geometry}, III.7) is:
484	
485	\begin{enumerate}
486	  \item The local ring $\mathcal{O}_{S,x}$ is a regular Noetherian local ring of Krull
487	    dimension $2$ (because $S$ is regular and $\dim S = 2$).
488	  \item By Corollary~\ref{cor:regular_cohen_macaulay} it is Cohen--Macaulay, so
489	    $\text{depth}(\mathcal{O}_{S,x}) = 2$. Equivalently, the maximal ideal contains an
490	    $\mathcal{O}_{S,x}$-regular sequence of length $2$ (any two transverse generators).
491	  \item Sections of the structure sheaf $\mathcal{O}_S$ on $U = S \setminus \{x\}$ extend
492	    uniquely across $x$ because $H^0_x(\mathcal{O}_S) = 0$ and
493	    $H^1_x(\mathcal{O}_S) = 0$ (these local-cohomology vanishings are equivalent to depth
494	    $\geq 2$ at $x$ by Stacks tag 0AVF; see Hartshorne, III.3.5, for the surface case).
495	  \item For a rational map $f : S \dashrightarrow A$ with codimension-$\geq 2$ indeterminacy
496	    locus on a regular surface, the same depth-$\geq 2$ input produces a sheaf-theoretic
497	    extension of $f$ to all of $S$: pull back any sheaf $\mathcal{O}_A$-affine-locally,
498	    obtain sections over $U$, and extend across $x$ by the above.
499	\end{enumerate}
500	
501	So Corollary~\ref{cor:regular_cohen_macaulay} is the gating input: the geometric A.4.a
502	proof can be written conditional on the algebraic Auslander--Buchsbaum corollary, and the
503	two halves develop independently --- as long as Theorem~\ref{thm:auslander_buchsbaum} and
504	Corollary~\ref{cor:regular_cohen_macaulay} are formalized in
505	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean} with the signatures pinned
506	in this chapter, A.4.a can consume them as Mathlib-style imports.
507	
508	\section{Lean encoding}
509	\label{sec:ab_lean_encoding}
510	
511	The Lean target file is the new \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean}.
512	Mathlib at the project's pinned commit (\texttt{b80f227}) already exposes substantial parts
513	of the depth / projective-dimension API:
514	\begin{itemize}
515	  \item \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.RegularSequence} provides
516	    \texttt{RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular} (definition of $M$-regular sequences),
517	    matching Definition~\ref{def:depth}.
518	  \item \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Depth} (if present at the pinned commit) provides
519	    \texttt{Module.depth} as the supremum of regular-sequence lengths; the prover
520	    should re-export this as \texttt{RingTheory.Module.depth} rather than re-define.
521	  \item \texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Abelian.ProjectiveDimension} (or
522	    \texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.ProjectiveResolution}) provides
523	    \texttt{Module.projectiveDimension} via finite projective resolutions, matching
524	    Definition~\ref{def:projective_dimension}.
525	  \item \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.CohenMacaulay} (in some recent revisions) provides
526	    \texttt{IsCohenMacaulayLocalRing}, matching Definition~\ref{def:cohen_macaulay_local}.
527	\end{itemize}
528	The Auslander--Buchsbaum formula itself (Theorem~\ref{thm:auslander_buchsbaum}) is the
529	content the project must supply unless Mathlib has it at the pinned commit (the Plan Agent
530	should confirm via \texttt{lean\_local\_search} on \texttt{auslander\_buchsbaum} /
531	\texttt{Module.auslanderBuchsbaum} / similar). If absent, the file will:
532	\begin{enumerate}
533	  \item State Theorem~\ref{thm:auslander_buchsbaum} as the formula
534	    $\text{pd}_R(M) + \text{depth}_R(M) = \text{depth}(R)$ under the hypotheses
535	    ``$(R, \mathfrak{m})$ Noetherian local, $M$ finitely generated, $\text{pd}_R(M) < \infty$,
536	    $M \neq 0$''.
537	  \item Prove it by induction on $\text{depth}(M)$ following the structure of the
538	    proof of Theorem~\ref{thm:auslander_buchsbaum} above, importing the supporting
539	    Lemma~\ref{lem:depth_short_exact_sequence} from Mathlib's depth API or
540	    proving it in tandem.
541	  \item Export Corollary~\ref{cor:regular_cohen_macaulay} as
542	    \texttt{RingTheory.CohenMacaulay.of\_regular} (or whatever name Mathlib uses if
543	    already present) as the consumer-facing statement.
544	\end{enumerate}
545	No new core axiom is required; the entire chapter is a homological-algebra construction.
546	The chapter is independent of the rest of Route A's geometric infrastructure (cube of
547	abelian varieties, theorem-of-the-square, Albanese universal property), making it a good
548	parallel-work candidate.
549	
550	\section{Out of scope}
551	\label{sec:ab_out_of_scope}
552	
553	This chapter packages \emph{only} the Auslander--Buchsbaum formula and the regular
554	$\Rightarrow$ Cohen--Macaulay corollary as required by A.4.a. The following downstream
555	constructions are explicitly out of scope here:
556	\begin{itemize}
557	  \item \textbf{The A.4.a codim-1-to-codim-2 extension proof itself} --- consumes
558	    Corollary~\ref{cor:regular_cohen_macaulay}, but the geometric extension argument
559	    lives in its sibling chapter \texttt{Albanese\_CodimExtension.tex} (planned).
560	  \item \textbf{Local-cohomology vanishing $H^i_x(\mathcal{O}_S) = 0$ for $i < \text{depth}$}
561	    --- a downstream consequence of Stacks tag 0AVF; planned for its own chapter
562	    \texttt{Albanese\_LocalCohomology.tex} or as part of A.4.a directly.
563	  \item \textbf{Global Cohen--Macaulay schemes / global depth} --- this chapter only treats
564	    the local-ring case. The global-to-local reduction is standard but not packaged here.
565	  \item \textbf{General homological-dimension theory (global dimension, finitistic
566	    dimension)} --- only the local-ring projective-dimension invariant appears here.
567	  \item \textbf{Cohen--Macaulay characterisations via $\omega_R$, dualising complex,
568	    Gorenstein refinement} --- the formula and its immediate Cohen--Macaulay corollary
569	    suffice for A.4.a; the duality side is not needed.
570	\end{itemize}

125	  mk₅ (AddCommGrpCat.ofHom ((mk₀ S.f).postcomp X (add_zero n₀)))
126	    (AddCommGrpCat.ofHom ((mk₀ S.g).postcomp X (add_zero n₀)))
127	    (AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (hS.extClass.postcomp X h))
128	    (AddCommGrpCat.ofHom ((mk₀ S.f).postcomp X (add_zero n₁)))
129	    (AddCommGrpCat.ofHom ((mk₀ S.g).postcomp X (add_zero n₁)))

134	    (exact_of_δ₀ (covariant_sequence_exact₃' X hS n₀ n₁ h).exact_toComposableArrows
135	      (exact_of_δ₀ (covariant_sequence_exact₁' X hS n₀ n₁ h).exact_toComposableArrows

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.ShortComplex.ModuleCat
9	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.QuotSMulTop
10	
11	/-!
12	# Categorical constructions for `IsSMulRegular`
13	-/
14	
15	@[expose] public section
16	
17	universe u v w
18	
19	variable {R : Type u} [CommRing R] (M : ModuleCat.{v} R)
20	
21	open CategoryTheory Ideal Pointwise
22	
23	lemma LinearMap.exact_smul_id_smul_top_mkQ (M : Type v) [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] (r : R) :
24	    Function.Exact (r • LinearMap.id : M →ₗ[R] M) (r • (⊤ : Submodule R M)).mkQ := by
25	  intro x
26	  simp [Submodule.mem_smul_pointwise_iff_exists,
27	    Submodule.mem_smul_pointwise_iff_exists]
28	
29	namespace ModuleCat
30	
31	/-- The short (exact) complex `M → M → M⧸xM` obtain from the scalar multiple of `x : R` on `M`. -/
32	@[simps]
33	def smulShortComplex (r : R) :
34	    ShortComplex (ModuleCat R) where
35	  X₁ := M
36	  X₂ := M
37	  X₃ := ModuleCat.of R (QuotSMulTop r M)
38	  f := ModuleCat.ofHom (r • LinearMap.id)
39	  g := ModuleCat.ofHom (r • (⊤ : Submodule R M)).mkQ
40	  zero := by
41	    ext x
42	    exact (LinearMap.exact_smul_id_smul_top_mkQ M r).apply_apply_eq_zero x
43	
44	lemma smulShortComplex_exact (r : R) : (smulShortComplex M r).Exact := by
45	  simp [smulShortComplex, ShortComplex.ShortExact.moduleCat_exact_iff_function_exact,
46	    LinearMap.exact_smul_id_smul_top_mkQ, -LinearMap.coe_smul]
47	
48	instance smulShortComplex_g_epi (r : R) : Epi (smulShortComplex M r).g := by
49	  simpa [smulShortComplex, ModuleCat.epi_iff_surjective] using Submodule.mkQ_surjective _
50	
51	end ModuleCat
52	
53	variable {M} in
54	lemma IsSMulRegular.smulShortComplex_shortExact {r : R} (reg : IsSMulRegular M r) :
55	    (ModuleCat.smulShortComplex M r).ShortExact where
56	  exact := ModuleCat.smulShortComplex_exact M r
57	  mono_f := by simpa [ModuleCat.smulShortComplex, ModuleCat.mono_iff_injective] using reg

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Artinian.Module
9	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.LocalRing.MaximalIdeal.Basic
10	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Nakayama
11	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Regular.IsSMulRegular
12	
13	/-!
14	# Regular sequences and weakly regular sequences
15	
16	The notion of a regular sequence is fundamental in commutative algebra.
17	Properties of regular sequences encode information about singularities of a
18	ring and regularity of a sequence can be tested homologically.
19	However the notion of a regular sequence is only really sensible for Noetherian local rings.
20	
21	TODO: Koszul regular sequences, `H_1`-regular sequences, quasi-regular sequences, depth.
22	
23	## Tags
24	
25	module, regular element, regular sequence, commutative algebra
26	-/
27	
28	@[expose] public section
29	
30	universe u v
31	
32	open scoped Pointwise
33	
34	variable {R S M M₂ M₃ M₄ : Type*}
35	
36	namespace Ideal
37	
38	variable [Semiring R] [Semiring S]
39	
40	/-- The ideal generated by a list of elements. -/
41	abbrev ofList (rs : List R) := span { r | r ∈ rs }
42	
43	@[simp] lemma ofList_nil : (ofList [] : Ideal R) = ⊥ :=
44	  have : { r | r ∈ [] } = ∅ := Set.eq_empty_of_forall_notMem (fun _ => List.not_mem_nil)
45	  Eq.trans (congrArg span this) span_empty
46	
47	@[simp] lemma ofList_append (rs₁ rs₂ : List R) :
48	    ofList (rs₁ ++ rs₂) = ofList rs₁ ⊔ ofList rs₂ :=
49	  have : { r | r ∈ rs₁ ++ rs₂ } = _ := Set.ext (fun _ => List.mem_append)
50	  Eq.trans (congrArg span this) (span_union _ _)
51	
52	lemma ofList_singleton (r : R) : ofList [r] = span {r} :=
53	  congrArg span (Set.ext fun _ => List.mem_singleton)
54	
55	@[simp] lemma ofList_cons (r : R) (rs : List R) :
56	    ofList (r::rs) = span {r} ⊔ ofList rs :=
57	  Eq.trans (ofList_append [r] rs) (congrArg (· ⊔ _) (ofList_singleton r))
58	
59	@[simp] lemma map_ofList (f : R →+* S) (rs : List R) :
60	    map f (ofList rs) = ofList (rs.map f) :=
61	  Eq.trans (map_span f { r | r ∈ rs }) <| congrArg span <|
62	    Set.ext (fun _ => List.mem_map.symm)
63	
64	lemma ofList_cons_smul {R} [CommSemiring R] (r : R) (rs : List R) {M}
65	    [AddCommMonoid M] [Module R M] (N : Submodule R M) :
66	    ofList (r :: rs) • N = r • N ⊔ ofList rs • N := by
67	  rw [ofList_cons, Submodule.sup_smul, Submodule.ideal_span_singleton_smul]
68	
69	end Ideal
70	
71	namespace Submodule
72	
73	variable (M) [CommRing R] [AddCommGroup M] [AddCommGroup M₂]
74	    [Module R M] [Module R M₂] (r : R) (rs : List R)
75	
76	/-- The equivalence between M ⧸ (r₀, r₁, …, rₙ)M and (M ⧸ r₀M) ⧸ (r₁, …, rₙ) (M ⧸ r₀M). -/
77	def quotOfListConsSMulTopEquivQuotSMulTopInner :
78	    (M ⧸ (Ideal.ofList (r :: rs) • ⊤ : Submodule R M)) ≃ₗ[R]
79	      QuotSMulTop r M ⧸ (Ideal.ofList rs • ⊤ : Submodule R (QuotSMulTop r M)) :=
80	  quotEquivOfEq _ _ (Ideal.ofList_cons_smul r rs ⊤) ≪≫ₗ
81	    (quotientQuotientEquivQuotientSup (r • ⊤) (Ideal.ofList rs • ⊤)).symm ≪≫ₗ
82	      quotEquivOfEq _ _ (by rw [map_smul'', map_top, range_mkQ])
83	
84	/-- The equivalence between M ⧸ (r₀, r₁, …, rₙ)M and (M ⧸ (r₁, …, rₙ)) ⧸ r₀ (M ⧸ (r₁, …, rₙ)). -/
85	def quotOfListConsSMulTopEquivQuotSMulTopOuter :
86	    (M ⧸ (Ideal.ofList (r :: rs) • ⊤ : Submodule R M)) ≃ₗ[R]
87	      QuotSMulTop r (M ⧸ (Ideal.ofList rs • ⊤ : Submodule R M)) :=
88	  quotEquivOfEq _ _ (Eq.trans (Ideal.ofList_cons_smul r rs ⊤) (sup_comm _ _)) ≪≫ₗ
89	    (quotientQuotientEquivQuotientSup (Ideal.ofList rs • ⊤) (r • ⊤)).symm ≪≫ₗ
90	      quotEquivOfEq _ _ (by rw [map_pointwise_smul, map_top, range_mkQ])
91	
92	variable {M}
93	
94	lemma quotOfListConsSMulTopEquivQuotSMulTopInner_naturality (f : M →ₗ[R] M₂) :
95	    (quotOfListConsSMulTopEquivQuotSMulTopInner M₂ r rs).toLinearMap ∘ₗ
96	        mapQ _ _ _ (smul_top_le_comap_smul_top (Ideal.ofList (r :: rs)) f) =
97	      mapQ _ _ _ (smul_top_le_comap_smul_top _ (QuotSMulTop.map r f)) ∘ₗ
98	        (quotOfListConsSMulTopEquivQuotSMulTopInner M r rs).toLinearMap :=
99	  quot_hom_ext _ _ _ fun _ => rfl
100	
101	lemma top_eq_ofList_cons_smul_iff :
102	    (⊤ : Submodule R M) = Ideal.ofList (r :: rs) • ⊤ ↔
103	      (⊤ : Submodule R (QuotSMulTop r M)) = Ideal.ofList rs • ⊤ := by
104	  conv => congr <;> rw [eq_comm, ← Quotient.subsingleton_iff]
105	  exact (quotOfListConsSMulTopEquivQuotSMulTopInner M r rs).toEquiv.subsingleton_congr
106	
107	end Submodule

109	namespace RingTheory.Sequence
110	
111	open scoped TensorProduct List
112	open Function Submodule QuotSMulTop
113	
114	variable (S M)
115	
116	section Definitions
117	
118	/-
119	In theory, regularity of `rs : List α` on `M` makes sense as soon as
120	`[Monoid α]`, `[AddCommGroup M]`, and `[DistribMulAction α M]`.
121	Instead of `Ideal.ofList (rs.take i) • (⊤ : Submodule R M)` we use
122	`⨆ (j : Fin i), rs[j] • (⊤ : AddSubgroup M)`.
123	However it's not clear that this is a useful generalization.
124	If we add the assumption `[SMulCommClass α α M]` this is essentially the same
125	as focusing on the commutative ring case, by passing to the monoid ring
126	`ℤ[abelianization of α]`.
127	-/
128	variable [CommRing R] [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M]
129	
130	open Ideal
131	
132	/-- A sequence `[r₁, …, rₙ]` is weakly regular on `M` iff `rᵢ` is regular on
133	`M⧸(r₁, …, rᵢ₋₁)M` for all `1 ≤ i ≤ n`. -/
134	@[mk_iff]
135	structure IsWeaklyRegular (rs : List R) : Prop where
136	  regular_mod_prev : ∀ i (h : i < rs.length),
137	    IsSMulRegular (M ⧸ (ofList (rs.take i) • ⊤ : Submodule R M)) rs[i]
138	
139	lemma isWeaklyRegular_iff_Fin (rs : List R) :
140	    IsWeaklyRegular M rs ↔ ∀ (i : Fin rs.length),
141	      IsSMulRegular (M ⧸ (ofList (rs.take i) • ⊤ : Submodule R M)) rs[i] :=
142	  Iff.trans (isWeaklyRegular_iff M rs) (Iff.symm Fin.forall_iff)
143	
144	/-- A weakly regular sequence `rs` on `M` is regular if also `M/rsM ≠ 0`. -/
145	@[mk_iff]
146	structure IsRegular (rs : List R) : Prop extends IsWeaklyRegular M rs where
147	  top_ne_smul : (⊤ : Submodule R M) ≠ Ideal.ofList rs • ⊤
148	
149	end Definitions
150	
151	section Congr
152	
153	variable {S M} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [AddCommGroup M] [AddCommGroup M₂]
154	    [Module R M] [Module S M₂]
155	    {σ : R →+* S} {σ' : S →+* R} [RingHomInvPair σ σ'] [RingHomInvPair σ' σ]
156	
157	open DistribMulAction AddSubgroup in
158	private lemma _root_.AddHom.map_smul_top_toAddSubgroup_of_surjective
159	    {f : M →+ M₂} {as : List R} {bs : List S} (hf : Function.Surjective f)
160	    (h : List.Forall₂ (fun r s => ∀ x, f (r • x) = s • f x) as bs) :
161	    (Ideal.ofList as • ⊤ : Submodule R M).toAddSubgroup.map f =
162	      (Ideal.ofList bs • ⊤ : Submodule S M₂).toAddSubgroup := by
163	  induction h with
164	  | nil =>
165	    convert AddSubgroup.map_bot f using 1 <;>
166	      rw [Ideal.ofList_nil, bot_smul, bot_toAddSubgroup]
167	  | @cons r s _ _ h _ ih =>
168	    conv => congr <;> rw [Ideal.ofList_cons, sup_smul, sup_toAddSubgroup,
169	      ideal_span_singleton_smul, pointwise_smul_toAddSubgroup,
170	      top_toAddSubgroup, AddSubgroup.pointwise_smul_def]
171	    apply DFunLike.ext (f.comp (toAddMonoidEnd R M r))
172	      ((toAddMonoidEnd S M₂ s).comp f) at h
173	    rw [AddSubgroup.map_sup, ih, map_map, h, ← map_map,
174	      map_top_of_surjective f hf]
175	
176	lemma _root_.AddEquiv.isWeaklyRegular_congr {e : M ≃+ M₂} {as bs}
177	    (h : List.Forall₂ (fun (r : R) (s : S) => ∀ x, e (r • x) = s • e x) as bs) :
178	    IsWeaklyRegular M as ↔ IsWeaklyRegular M₂ bs := by
179	  conv => congr <;> rw [isWeaklyRegular_iff_Fin]
180	  let e' i : (M ⧸ (Ideal.ofList (as.take i) • ⊤ : Submodule R M)) ≃+
181	      M₂ ⧸ (Ideal.ofList (bs.take i) • ⊤ : Submodule S M₂) :=
182	    QuotientAddGroup.congr _ _ e <|
183	      AddHom.map_smul_top_toAddSubgroup_of_surjective e.surjective <|
184	        List.forall₂_take i h
185	  refine (finCongr h.length_eq).forall_congr @fun _ => (e' _).isSMulRegular_congr ?_
186	  exact (mkQ_surjective _).forall.mpr fun _ => congrArg (mkQ _) (h.get _ _ _)
187	
188	lemma _root_.LinearEquiv.isWeaklyRegular_congr' (e : M ≃ₛₗ[σ] M₂) (rs : List R) :
189	    IsWeaklyRegular M rs ↔ IsWeaklyRegular M₂ (rs.map σ) :=
190	  e.toAddEquiv.isWeaklyRegular_congr <| List.forall₂_map_right_iff.mpr <|
191	    List.forall₂_same.mpr fun r _ x => e.map_smul' r x
192	
193	lemma _root_.LinearEquiv.isWeaklyRegular_congr [Module R M₂] (e : M ≃ₗ[R] M₂) (rs : List R) :
194	    IsWeaklyRegular M rs ↔ IsWeaklyRegular M₂ rs :=
195	  Iff.trans (e.isWeaklyRegular_congr' rs) <| iff_of_eq <| congrArg _ rs.map_id
196	
197	lemma _root_.AddEquiv.isRegular_congr {e : M ≃+ M₂} {as bs}
198	    (h : List.Forall₂ (fun (r : R) (s : S) => ∀ x, e (r • x) = s • e x) as bs) :
199	    IsRegular M as ↔ IsRegular M₂ bs := by
200	  conv => congr <;> rw [isRegular_iff, ne_comm, ← Quotient.nontrivial_iff]
201	  let e' := QuotientAddGroup.congr _ _ e <|
202	    AddHom.map_smul_top_toAddSubgroup_of_surjective e.surjective h
203	  exact and_congr (e.isWeaklyRegular_congr h) e'.nontrivial_congr
204	
205	lemma _root_.LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr' (e : M ≃ₛₗ[σ] M₂) (rs : List R) :
206	    IsRegular M rs ↔ IsRegular M₂ (rs.map σ) :=
207	  e.toAddEquiv.isRegular_congr <| List.forall₂_map_right_iff.mpr <|
208	    List.forall₂_same.mpr fun r _ x => e.map_smul' r x
209	
210	lemma _root_.LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr [Module R M₂] (e : M ≃ₗ[R] M₂) (rs : List R) :
211	    IsRegular M rs ↔ IsRegular M₂ rs :=
212	  Iff.trans (e.isRegular_congr' rs) <| iff_of_eq <| congrArg _ rs.map_id
213	
214	end Congr
215	
216	lemma isWeaklyRegular_map_algebraMap_iff [CommRing R] [CommRing S]
217	    [Algebra R S] [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [Module S M]
218	    [IsScalarTower R S M] (rs : List R) :
219	    IsWeaklyRegular M (rs.map (algebraMap R S)) ↔ IsWeaklyRegular M rs :=
220	  (AddEquiv.refl M).isWeaklyRegular_congr <| List.forall₂_map_left_iff.mpr <|
221	    List.forall₂_same.mpr fun r _ => algebraMap_smul S r
222	
223	variable [CommRing R] [AddCommGroup M] [AddCommGroup M₂] [AddCommGroup M₃]
224	    [AddCommGroup M₄] [Module R M] [Module R M₂] [Module R M₃] [Module R M₄]
225	
226	@[simp]
227	lemma isWeaklyRegular_cons_iff (r : R) (rs : List R) :
228	    IsWeaklyRegular M (r :: rs) ↔
229	      IsSMulRegular M r ∧ IsWeaklyRegular (QuotSMulTop r M) rs :=
230	  have := Eq.trans (congrArg (· • ⊤) Ideal.ofList_nil) (bot_smul ⊤)
231	  let e i := quotOfListConsSMulTopEquivQuotSMulTopInner M r (rs.take i)
232	  Iff.trans (isWeaklyRegular_iff_Fin _ _) <| Iff.trans Fin.forall_iff_succ <|
233	    and_congr ((quotEquivOfEqBot _ this).isSMulRegular_congr r) <|
234	      Iff.trans (forall_congr' fun i => (e i).isSMulRegular_congr (rs.get i))
235	        (isWeaklyRegular_iff_Fin _ _).symm
236	
237	lemma isWeaklyRegular_cons_iff' (r : R) (rs : List R) :
238	    IsWeaklyRegular M (r :: rs) ↔
239	      IsSMulRegular M r ∧
240	        IsWeaklyRegular (QuotSMulTop r M)
241	          (rs.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {r}))) :=
242	  Iff.trans (isWeaklyRegular_cons_iff M r rs) <| and_congr_right' <|
243	    Iff.symm <| isWeaklyRegular_map_algebraMap_iff (R ⧸ Ideal.span {r}) _ rs
244	
245	@[simp]
246	lemma isRegular_cons_iff (r : R) (rs : List R) :
247	    IsRegular M (r :: rs) ↔
248	      IsSMulRegular M r ∧ IsRegular (QuotSMulTop r M) rs := by
249	  rw [isRegular_iff, isRegular_iff, isWeaklyRegular_cons_iff M r rs,
250	    ne_eq, top_eq_ofList_cons_smul_iff, and_assoc]
251	
252	lemma isRegular_cons_iff' (r : R) (rs : List R) :
253	    IsRegular M (r :: rs) ↔
254	      IsSMulRegular M r ∧ IsRegular (QuotSMulTop r M)
255	          (rs.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {r}))) := by
256	  conv => congr <;> rw [isRegular_iff, ne_eq]
257	  rw [isWeaklyRegular_cons_iff', ← restrictScalars_inj R (R ⧸ _),
258	    ← Ideal.map_ofList, ← Ideal.Quotient.algebraMap_eq, Ideal.smul_restrictScalars,
259	    restrictScalars_top, top_eq_ofList_cons_smul_iff, and_assoc]
260	
261	variable {M}
262	
263	namespace IsWeaklyRegular
264	
265	variable (R M) in
266	@[simp] lemma nil : IsWeaklyRegular M ([] : List R) :=
267	  .mk (False.elim <| Nat.not_lt_zero · ·)
268	
269	lemma cons {r : R} {rs : List R} (h1 : IsSMulRegular M r)
270	    (h2 : IsWeaklyRegular (QuotSMulTop r M) rs) : IsWeaklyRegular M (r :: rs) :=
271	  (isWeaklyRegular_cons_iff M r rs).mpr ⟨h1, h2⟩
272	
273	lemma cons' {r : R} {rs : List R} (h1 : IsSMulRegular M r)
274	    (h2 : IsWeaklyRegular (QuotSMulTop r M)
275	            (rs.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {r})))) :
276	    IsWeaklyRegular M (r :: rs) :=
277	  (isWeaklyRegular_cons_iff' M r rs).mpr ⟨h1, h2⟩
278	
279	/-- Weakly regular sequences can be inductively characterized by:
280	* The empty sequence is weakly regular on any module.
281	* If `r` is regular on `M` and `rs` is a weakly regular sequence on `M⧸rM` then
282	  the sequence obtained from `rs` by prepending `r` is weakly regular on `M`.
283	
284	This is the induction principle produced by the inductive definition above.
285	The motive will usually be valued in `Prop`, but `Sort*` works too. -/
286	@[induction_eliminator]
287	def recIterModByRegular
288	    {motive : (M : Type v) → [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → (rs : List R) →
289	      IsWeaklyRegular M rs → Sort*}
290	    (nil : (M : Type v) → [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → motive M [] (nil R M))
291	    (cons : {M : Type v} → [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → (r : R) →
292	      (rs : List R) → (h1 : IsSMulRegular M r) →
293	      (h2 : IsWeaklyRegular (QuotSMulTop r M) rs) →
294	      (ih : motive (QuotSMulTop r M) rs h2) → motive M (r :: rs) (cons h1 h2)) :
295	    {M : Type v} → [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → {rs : List R} →
296	    (h : IsWeaklyRegular M rs) → motive M rs h
297	  | M, _, _, [], _ => nil M
298	  | M, _, _, r :: rs, h =>
299	    let ⟨h1, h2⟩ := (isWeaklyRegular_cons_iff M r rs).mp h
300	    cons r rs h1 h2 (recIterModByRegular nil cons h2)
301	
302	/-- A simplified version of `IsWeaklyRegular.recIterModByRegular` where the
303	motive is not allowed to depend on the proof of `IsWeaklyRegular`. -/
304	def ndrecIterModByRegular
305	    {motive : (M : Type v) → [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → (rs : List R) → Sort*}
306	    (nil : (M : Type v) → [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → motive M [])
307	    (cons : {M : Type v} → [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → (r : R) →
308	      (rs : List R) → IsSMulRegular M r → IsWeaklyRegular (QuotSMulTop r M) rs →
309	      motive (QuotSMulTop r M) rs → motive M (r :: rs))
310	    {M} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] {rs} :
311	    IsWeaklyRegular M rs → motive M rs :=
312	  recIterModByRegular (motive := fun M _ _ rs _ => motive M rs) nil cons
313	
314	/-- An alternate induction principle from `IsWeaklyRegular.recIterModByRegular`
315	where we mod out by successive elements in both the module and the base ring.
316	This is useful for propagating certain properties of the initial `M`, e.g.
317	faithfulness or freeness, throughout the induction. -/
318	def recIterModByRegularWithRing
319	    {motive : (R : Type u) → [CommRing R] → (M : Type v) → [AddCommGroup M] →
320	      [Module R M] → (rs : List R) → IsWeaklyRegular M rs → Sort*}
321	    (nil : (R : Type u) → [CommRing R] → (M : Type v) → [AddCommGroup M] →
322	      [Module R M] → motive R M [] (nil R M))
323	    (cons : {R : Type u} → [CommRing R] → {M : Type v} → [AddCommGroup M] →
324	      [Module R M] → (r : R) → (rs : List R) → (h1 : IsSMulRegular M r) →
325	      (h2 : IsWeaklyRegular (QuotSMulTop r M)
326	              (rs.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {r})))) →
327	      (ih : motive (R ⧸ Ideal.span {r}) (QuotSMulTop r M)
328	              (rs.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {r}))) h2) →
329	            motive R M (r :: rs) (cons' h1 h2)) :
330	    {R : Type u} → [CommRing R] → {M : Type v} → [AddCommGroup M] →
331	    [Module R M] → {rs : List R} → (h : IsWeaklyRegular M rs) → motive R M rs h
332	  | R, _, M, _, _, [], _ => nil R M
333	  | _, _, M, _, _, r :: rs, h =>
334	    let ⟨h1, h2⟩ := (isWeaklyRegular_cons_iff' M r rs).mp h
335	    cons r rs h1 h2 (recIterModByRegularWithRing nil cons h2)
336	  termination_by _ _ _ _ _ rs => List.length rs
337	
338	/-- A simplified version of `IsWeaklyRegular.recIterModByRegularWithRing` where
339	the motive is not allowed to depend on the proof of `IsWeaklyRegular`. -/
340	def ndrecWithRing
341	    {motive : (R : Type u) → [CommRing R] → (M : Type v) →
342	      [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → (rs : List R) → Sort*}
343	    (nil : (R : Type u) → [CommRing R] → (M : Type v) →
344	      [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → motive R M [])
345	    (cons : {R : Type u} → [CommRing R] → {M : Type v} → [AddCommGroup M] →
346	      [Module R M] → (r : R) → (rs : List R) → IsSMulRegular M r →
347	      IsWeaklyRegular (QuotSMulTop r M)
348	        (rs.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {r}))) →
349	      motive (R ⧸ Ideal.span {r}) (QuotSMulTop r M)
350	        (rs.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {r}))) → motive R M (r :: rs))
351	    {R} [CommRing R] {M} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] {rs} :
352	    IsWeaklyRegular M rs → motive R M rs :=
353	  recIterModByRegularWithRing (motive := fun R _ M _ _ rs _ => motive R M rs)
354	    nil cons
355	
356	end IsWeaklyRegular
357	
358	section
359	
360	variable (M)
361	
362	lemma isWeaklyRegular_singleton_iff (r : R) :
363	    IsWeaklyRegular M [r] ↔ IsSMulRegular M r :=
364	  Iff.trans (isWeaklyRegular_cons_iff M r []) (and_iff_left (.nil R _))
365	
366	lemma isWeaklyRegular_append_iff (rs₁ rs₂ : List R) :
367	    IsWeaklyRegular M (rs₁ ++ rs₂) ↔
368	      IsWeaklyRegular M rs₁ ∧
369	        IsWeaklyRegular (M ⧸ (Ideal.ofList rs₁ • ⊤ : Submodule R M)) rs₂ := by
370	  induction rs₁ generalizing M with
371	  | nil =>
372	    refine Iff.symm <| Iff.trans (and_iff_right (.nil R M)) ?_
373	    refine (quotEquivOfEqBot _ ?_).isWeaklyRegular_congr rs₂
374	    rw [Ideal.ofList_nil, bot_smul]
375	  | cons r rs₁ ih =>
376	    let e := quotOfListConsSMulTopEquivQuotSMulTopInner M r rs₁
377	    rw [List.cons_append, isWeaklyRegular_cons_iff, isWeaklyRegular_cons_iff,
378	      ih, ← and_assoc, ← e.isWeaklyRegular_congr rs₂]
379	
380	lemma isWeaklyRegular_append_iff' (rs₁ rs₂ : List R) :
381	    IsWeaklyRegular M (rs₁ ++ rs₂) ↔
382	      IsWeaklyRegular M rs₁ ∧
383	        IsWeaklyRegular (M ⧸ (Ideal.ofList rs₁ • ⊤ : Submodule R M))
384	          (rs₂.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.ofList rs₁))) :=
385	  Iff.trans (isWeaklyRegular_append_iff M rs₁ rs₂) <| and_congr_right' <|
386	    Iff.symm <| isWeaklyRegular_map_algebraMap_iff (R ⧸ Ideal.ofList rs₁) _ rs₂
387	
388	end
389	
390	namespace IsRegular
391	
392	variable (R M) in
393	lemma nil [Nontrivial M] : IsRegular M ([] : List R) where
394	  toIsWeaklyRegular := IsWeaklyRegular.nil R M
395	  top_ne_smul h := by
396	    rw [Ideal.ofList_nil, bot_smul, eq_comm, subsingleton_iff_bot_eq_top] at h
397	    exact not_subsingleton M ((Submodule.subsingleton_iff _).mp h)
398	
399	lemma cons {r : R} {rs : List R} (h1 : IsSMulRegular M r)
400	    (h2 : IsRegular (QuotSMulTop r M) rs) : IsRegular M (r :: rs) :=
401	  (isRegular_cons_iff M r rs).mpr ⟨h1, h2⟩
402	
403	lemma cons' {r : R} {rs : List R} (h1 : IsSMulRegular M r)
404	    (h2 : IsRegular (QuotSMulTop r M) (rs.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {r})))) :
405	    IsRegular M (r :: rs) :=
406	  (isRegular_cons_iff' M r rs).mpr ⟨h1, h2⟩
407	
408	/-- Regular sequences can be inductively characterized by:
409	* The empty sequence is regular on any nonzero module.
410	* If `r` is regular on `M` and `rs` is a regular sequence on `M⧸rM` then the
411	  sequence obtained from `rs` by prepending `r` is regular on `M`.
412	
413	This is the induction principle produced by the inductive definition above.
414	The motive will usually be valued in `Prop`, but `Sort*` works too. -/
415	@[induction_eliminator]
416	def recIterModByRegular
417	    {motive : (M : Type v) → [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → (rs : List R) →
418	      IsRegular M rs → Sort*}
419	    (nil : (M : Type v) → [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → [Nontrivial M] →
420	      motive M [] (nil R M))
421	    (cons : {M : Type v} → [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → (r : R) →
422	      (rs : List R) → (h1 : IsSMulRegular M r) → (h2 : IsRegular (QuotSMulTop r M) rs) →
423	      (ih : motive (QuotSMulTop r M) rs h2) → motive M (r :: rs) (cons h1 h2))
424	    {M} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] {rs} (h : IsRegular M rs) : motive M rs h :=
425	  h.toIsWeaklyRegular.recIterModByRegular
426	    (motive := fun N _ _ rs' h' => ∀ h'', motive N rs' ⟨h', h''⟩)
427	    (fun N _ _ h' =>
428	      haveI := (nontrivial_iff R).mp (nontrivial_of_ne _ _ h'); nil N)
429	    (fun r rs' h1 h2 h3 h4 =>
430	      have ⟨h5, h6⟩ := (isRegular_cons_iff _ _ _).mp ⟨h2.cons h1, h4⟩
431	      cons r rs' h5 h6 (h3 h6.top_ne_smul))
432	    h.top_ne_smul
433	
434	/-- A simplified version of `IsRegular.recIterModByRegular` where the motive is
435	not allowed to depend on the proof of `IsRegular`. -/
436	def ndrecIterModByRegular
437	    {motive : (M : Type v) → [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → (rs : List R) → Sort*}
438	    (nil : (M : Type v) → [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → [Nontrivial M] → motive M [])
439	    (cons : {M : Type v} → [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → (r : R) →
440	      (rs : List R) → IsSMulRegular M r → IsRegular (QuotSMulTop r M) rs →
441	      motive (QuotSMulTop r M) rs → motive M (r :: rs))
442	    {M} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] {rs} : IsRegular M rs → motive M rs :=
443	  recIterModByRegular (motive := fun M _ _ rs _ => motive M rs) nil cons
444	
445	/-- An alternate induction principle from `IsRegular.recIterModByRegular` where
446	we mod out by successive elements in both the module and the base ring. This is
447	useful for propagating certain properties of the initial `M`, e.g. faithfulness
448	or freeness, throughout the induction. -/
449	def recIterModByRegularWithRing
450	    {motive : (R : Type u) → [CommRing R] → (M : Type v) → [AddCommGroup M] →
451	      [Module R M] → (rs : List R) → IsRegular M rs → Sort*}
452	    (nil : (R : Type u) → [CommRing R] → (M : Type v) → [AddCommGroup M] →
453	      [Module R M] → [Nontrivial M] → motive R M [] (nil R M))
454	    (cons : {R : Type u} → [CommRing R] → {M : Type v} → [AddCommGroup M] →
455	      [Module R M] → (r : R) → (rs : List R) → (h1 : IsSMulRegular M r) →
456	      (h2 : IsRegular (QuotSMulTop r M)
457	              (rs.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {r})))) →
458	      (ih : motive (R ⧸ Ideal.span {r}) (QuotSMulTop r M)
459	              (rs.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {r}))) h2) →
460	            motive R M (r :: rs) (cons' h1 h2))
461	    {R} [CommRing R] {M} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] {rs}
462	    (h : IsRegular M rs) : motive R M rs h :=
463	  h.toIsWeaklyRegular.recIterModByRegularWithRing
464	    (motive := fun R _ N _ _ rs' h' => ∀ h'', motive R N rs' ⟨h', h''⟩)
465	    (fun R _ N _ _ h' =>
466	      haveI := (nontrivial_iff R).mp (nontrivial_of_ne _ _ h'); nil R N)
467	    (fun r rs' h1 h2 h3 h4 =>
468	      have ⟨h5, h6⟩ := (isRegular_cons_iff' _ _ _).mp ⟨h2.cons' h1, h4⟩
469	      cons r rs' h5 h6 <| h3 h6.top_ne_smul)
470	    h.top_ne_smul
471	
472	/-- A simplified version of `IsRegular.recIterModByRegularWithRing` where the
473	motive is not allowed to depend on the proof of `IsRegular`. -/
474	def ndrecIterModByRegularWithRing
475	    {motive : (R : Type u) → [CommRing R] → (M : Type v) →
476	      [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → (rs : List R) → Sort*}
477	    (nil : (R : Type u) → [CommRing R] → (M : Type v) →
478	      [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → [Nontrivial M] → motive R M [])
479	    (cons : {R : Type u} → [CommRing R] → {M : Type v} →
480	      [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → (r : R) → (rs : List R) →
481	      IsSMulRegular M r →
482	      IsRegular (QuotSMulTop r M)
483	        (rs.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {r}))) →
484	      motive (R ⧸ Ideal.span {r}) (QuotSMulTop r M)
485	        (rs.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {r}))) →
486	      motive R M (r :: rs))
487	    {R} [CommRing R] {M} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] {rs} :
488	    IsRegular M rs → motive R M rs :=
489	  recIterModByRegularWithRing (motive := fun R _ M _ _ rs _ => motive R M rs)
490	    nil cons

498	  haveI := quot_ofList_smul_nontrivial h ⊤
499	  (mkQ_surjective (Ideal.ofList rs • ⊤ : Submodule R M)).nontrivial

549	lemma map_first_exact_on_four_term_right_exact_of_isSMulRegular_last
550	    {rs : List R} {f₁ : M →ₗ[R] M₂} {f₂ : M₂ →ₗ[R] M₃} {f₃ : M₃ →ₗ[R] M₄}
551	    (h₁₂ : Exact f₁ f₂) (h₂₃ : Exact f₂ f₃) (h₃ : Surjective f₃)
552	    (h₄ : IsWeaklyRegular M₄ rs) :
553	    Exact (mapQ _ _ _ (smul_top_le_comap_smul_top (Ideal.ofList rs) f₁))
554	          (mapQ _ _ _ (smul_top_le_comap_smul_top (Ideal.ofList rs) f₂)) := by
555	  induction h₄ generalizing M M₂ M₃ with
556	  | nil =>
557	    apply (Exact.iff_of_ladder_linearEquiv ?_ ?_).mp h₁₂
558	    any_goals exact quotEquivOfEqBot _ <|
559	      Eq.trans (congrArg (· • ⊤) Ideal.ofList_nil) (bot_smul ⊤)
560	    all_goals exact quot_hom_ext _ _ _ fun _ => rfl
561	  | cons r rs h₄ _ ih =>
562	    specialize ih
563	      (map_first_exact_on_four_term_exact_of_isSMulRegular_last h₁₂ h₂₃ h₄)
564	      (map_exact r h₂₃ h₃) (map_surjective r h₃)
565	    have H₁ := quotOfListConsSMulTopEquivQuotSMulTopInner_naturality r rs f₁
566	    have H₂ := quotOfListConsSMulTopEquivQuotSMulTopInner_naturality r rs f₂

574	private lemma IsWeaklyRegular.swap {a b : R} (h1 : IsWeaklyRegular M [a, b])
575	    (h2 : torsionBy R M b = a • torsionBy R M b → torsionBy R M b = ⊥) :
576	    IsWeaklyRegular M [b, a] := by
577	  rw [isWeaklyRegular_cons_iff, isWeaklyRegular_singleton_iff] at h1 ⊢
578	  obtain ⟨ha, hb⟩ := h1
579	  rw [← isSMulRegular_iff_torsionBy_eq_bot] at h2
580	  specialize h2 (le_antisymm ?_ (smul_le_self_of_tower a (torsionBy R M b)))
581	  · refine le_of_eq_of_le ?_ <| smul_top_inf_eq_smul_of_isSMulRegular_on_quot <|
582	      ha.of_injective _ <| ker_eq_bot.mp <| ker_liftQ_eq_bot' _ (lsmul R M b) rfl
583	    rw [← (isSMulRegular_on_quot_iff_lsmul_comap_eq _ _).mp hb]
584	    exact (inf_eq_right.mpr (ker_le_comap _)).symm
585	  · rwa [ha.isSMulRegular_on_quot_iff_smul_top_inf_eq_smul, inf_comm, smul_comm,
586	      ← h2.isSMulRegular_on_quot_iff_smul_top_inf_eq_smul, and_iff_left hb]
587	
588	-- TODO: Equivalence of permutability of regular sequences to regularity of
589	-- subsequences and regularity on poly ring. See [07DW] in stacks project
590	-- We need a theory of multivariate polynomial modules first
591	
592	lemma IsWeaklyRegular.prototype_perm {rs : List R} (h : IsWeaklyRegular M rs)
593	    {rs'} (h'' : rs ~ rs') (h' : ∀ a b rs', (a :: b :: rs') <+~ rs →
594	      let K := torsionBy R (M ⧸ (Ideal.ofList rs' • ⊤ : Submodule R M)) b
595	      K = a • K → K = ⊥) : IsWeaklyRegular M rs' :=
596	  have H := LinearEquiv.isWeaklyRegular_congr <| quotEquivOfEqBot _ <|
597	    Eq.trans (congrArg (· • ⊤) Ideal.ofList_nil) (bot_smul ⊤)
598	  (H rs').mp <| (aux [] h'' (.refl rs) (h''.symm.subperm)) <| (H rs).mpr h
599	  where aux {rs₁ rs₂} (rs₀ : List R)
600	    (h₁₂ : rs₁ ~ rs₂) (H₁ : rs₀ ++ rs₁ <+~ rs) (H₃ : rs₀ ++ rs₂ <+~ rs)
601	    (h : IsWeaklyRegular (M ⧸ (Ideal.ofList rs₀ • ⊤ : Submodule R M)) rs₁) :
602	    IsWeaklyRegular (M ⧸ (Ideal.ofList rs₀ • ⊤ : Submodule R M)) rs₂ := by {
603	  induction h₁₂ generalizing rs₀ with
604	  | nil => exact .nil R _
605	  | cons r _ ih =>
606	    let e := quotOfListConsSMulTopEquivQuotSMulTopOuter M r rs₀
607	    rw [isWeaklyRegular_cons_iff, ← e.isWeaklyRegular_congr] at h ⊢
608	    refine h.imp_right (ih (r :: rs₀) ?_ ?_) <;>
609	      exact List.perm_middle.subperm_right.mp ‹_›
610	  | swap a b t =>
611	    rw [show ∀ x y z, x :: y :: z = [x, y] ++ z from fun _ _ _ => rfl,
612	      isWeaklyRegular_append_iff] at h ⊢
613	    have : Ideal.ofList [b, a] = Ideal.ofList [a, b] :=
614	      congrArg Ideal.span <| Set.ext fun _ => (List.Perm.swap a b []).mem_iff
615	    rw [(quotEquivOfEq _ _ (congrArg₂ _ this rfl)).isWeaklyRegular_congr] at h
616	    rw [List.append_cons, List.append_cons, List.append_assoc _ [b] [a]] at H₁
617	    apply (List.sublist_append_left (rs₀ ++ [b, a]) _).subperm.trans at H₁
618	    apply List.perm_append_comm.subperm.trans at H₁
619	    exact h.imp_left (swap · (h' b a rs₀ H₁))
620	  | trans h₁₂ _ ih₁₂ ih₂₃ =>
621	    have H₂ := (h₁₂.append_left rs₀).subperm_right.mp H₁
622	    exact ih₂₃ rs₀ H₂ H₃ (ih₁₂ rs₀ H₁ H₂ h) }
623	
624	-- putting `{rs' : List R}` and `h2` after `h3` would be better for partial
625	-- application, but this argument order seems nicer overall
626	lemma IsWeaklyRegular.of_perm_of_subset_jacobson_annihilator [IsNoetherian R M]
627	    {rs rs' : List R} (h1 : IsWeaklyRegular M rs) (h2 : List.Perm rs rs')
628	    (h3 : ∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ (Module.annihilator R M).jacobson) :
629	    IsWeaklyRegular M rs' :=
630	  h1.prototype_perm h2 fun r _ _ h h' =>
631	    eq_bot_of_eq_pointwise_smul_of_mem_jacobson_annihilator
632	      (IsNoetherian.noetherian _) h'
633	      (Ideal.jacobson_mono
634	        (le_trans
635	          -- The named argument `(R := R)` below isn't necessary, but
636	          -- typechecking is much slower without it
637	          (LinearMap.annihilator_le_of_surjective (R := R) _ (mkQ_surjective _))
638	          (LinearMap.annihilator_le_of_injective _ (injective_subtype _)))
639	        (h3 r (h.subset List.mem_cons_self)))
640	
641	end Perm

643	lemma IsRegular.of_perm_of_subset_jacobson_annihilator [IsNoetherian R M]
644	    {rs rs' : List R} (h1 : IsRegular M rs) (h2 : List.Perm rs rs')
645	    (h3 : ∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ (Module.annihilator R M).jacobson) : IsRegular M rs' :=
646	  ⟨h1.toIsWeaklyRegular.of_perm_of_subset_jacobson_annihilator h2 h3,
647	    letI := h1.nontrivial
648	    top_ne_ideal_smul_of_le_jacobson_annihilator <|
649	      Ideal.span_le.mpr (h3 · <| h2.mem_iff.mpr ·)⟩
650	
651	lemma _root_.IsLocalRing.isWeaklyRegular_of_perm_of_subset_maximalIdeal
652	    [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherian R M] {rs rs' : List R}
653	    (h1 : IsWeaklyRegular M rs) (h2 : List.Perm rs rs')
654	    (h3 : ∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) : IsWeaklyRegular M rs' :=
655	  IsWeaklyRegular.of_perm_of_subset_jacobson_annihilator h1 h2 fun r hr =>
656	    IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal_le_jacobson _ (h3 r hr)
657	
658	lemma _root_.IsLocalRing.isRegular_of_perm [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherian R M]
659	    {rs rs' : List R} (h1 : IsRegular M rs) (h2 : List.Perm rs rs') :
660	    IsRegular M rs' := by
661	  obtain ⟨h3, h4⟩ := h1
662	  refine ⟨IsLocalRing.isWeaklyRegular_of_perm_of_subset_maximalIdeal h3 h2 ?_, ?_⟩
663	  · intro x (h6 : x ∈ { r | r ∈ rs })
664	    refine IsLocalRing.le_maximalIdeal ?_ (Ideal.subset_span h6)
665	    exact h4 ∘ Eq.trans (top_smul _).symm ∘ Eq.symm ∘ congrArg (· • ⊤)
666	  · refine ne_of_ne_of_eq h4 (congrArg (Ideal.span · • ⊤) ?_)
667	    exact Set.ext fun _ => h2.mem_iff

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Module.FinitePresentation
9	public import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Dual.Lemmas
10	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.AssociatedPrime.Finiteness
11	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.AssociatedPrime.Localization
12	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.LocalRing.ResidueField.Ideal
13	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Regular.IsSMulRegular
14	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Support

16	/-!
17	
18	# Hom(N,M) is subsingleton iff there exists a smul regular element of M in ann(N)
19	
20	Let `M` and `N` be `R`-modules. In this section we prove that `Hom(N,M)` is subsingleton iff
21	there exist `r : R`, such that `IsSMulRegular M r` and `r ∈ ann(N)`.
22	This is the case if `Depth[I](M) = 0`.
23	
24	## Main statements
25	
26	* `IsSMulRegular.subsingleton_linearMap_iff` : for `R` module `N M`, `Hom(N, M) = 0`
27	  iff there is a `M`-regular in `Module.annihilator R N`.
28	
29	-/
30	
31	public section
32	
33	open IsLocalRing LinearMap Module
34	
35	namespace IsSMulRegular
36	
37	variable {R M N : Type*} [CommRing R] [AddCommGroup M] [AddCommGroup N] [Module R M] [Module R N]
38	
39	lemma linearMap_subsingleton_of_mem_annihilator {r : R} (reg : IsSMulRegular M r)
40	    (mem_ann : r ∈ Module.annihilator R N) : Subsingleton (N →ₗ[R] M) := by
41	  apply subsingleton_of_forall_eq 0 (fun f ↦ ext fun x ↦ ?_)
42	  have : r • (f x) = r • 0 := by
43	    rw [smul_zero, ← map_smul, Module.mem_annihilator.mp mem_ann x, map_zero]
44	  simpa using reg this
45	
46	lemma subsingleton_linearMap_iff [IsNoetherianRing R] [Module.Finite R M] [Module.Finite R N] :
47	    Subsingleton (N →ₗ[R] M) ↔ ∃ r ∈ Module.annihilator R N, IsSMulRegular M r := by
48	  refine ⟨fun hom0 ↦ ?_, fun ⟨r, mem_ann, reg⟩ ↦
49	    linearMap_subsingleton_of_mem_annihilator reg mem_ann⟩
50	  cases subsingleton_or_nontrivial M
51	  · exact ⟨0, ⟨Submodule.zero_mem (Module.annihilator R N), IsSMulRegular.zero⟩⟩
52	  · by_contra! h
53	    have hexist : ∃ p ∈ associatedPrimes R M, Module.annihilator R N ≤ p := by
54	      rcases associatedPrimes.nonempty R M with ⟨Ia, hIa⟩
55	      apply (Ideal.subset_union_prime_finite (associatedPrimes.finite R M) Ia Ia _).mp
56	      · rw [biUnion_associatedPrimes_eq_compl_regular R M]
57	        exact fun r hr ↦ h r hr
58	      · exact fun I hin _ _ ↦ IsAssociatedPrime.isPrime hin
59	    rcases hexist with ⟨p, pass, hp⟩
60	    let _ := pass.isPrime
61	    let p' : PrimeSpectrum R := ⟨p, pass.isPrime⟩
62	    have loc_ne_zero : p' ∈ Module.support R N := Module.mem_support_iff_of_finite.mpr hp
63	    rw [Module.mem_support_iff] at loc_ne_zero
64	    let Rₚ := Localization.AtPrime p
65	    let Nₚ := LocalizedModule.AtPrime p'.asIdeal N
66	    let Mₚ := LocalizedModule.AtPrime p'.asIdeal M
67	    let Nₚ' := Nₚ ⧸ (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (Localization.AtPrime p)) • (⊤ : Submodule Rₚ Nₚ)
68	    have ntr : Nontrivial Nₚ' :=
69	      Submodule.Quotient.nontrivial_iff.mpr <| .symm <|

-- iter-184 Lane G: closed via LES chase + sSup extraction + cons.
      --
      -- Step A: derive `∀ j < n, Ext^j(κ, MxM) = 0` from `hext` via the
      --   LES of `Ext^*(κ, ·)` on the SES `0 → M →[x] M → MxM → 0`.
      let S : ShortComplex (ModuleCat.{u} R) :=
        ModuleCat.smulShortComplex (ModuleCat.of R M) x
      have hS : S.ShortExact := hxReg.smulShortComplex_shortExact
      set κ : ModuleCat.{u} R := ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R)
        with hκ
      have hMxM_vanish : ∀ j < n, ∀ e : Abelian.Ext.{u} κ
          (ModuleCat.of R MxM) j, e = 0 := by
        intro j hj e
        -- `e.comp hS.extClass rfl : Ext κ M (j+1) = 0` by `hext` at `j+1`.
        have he_ext : e.comp hS.extClass (rfl : j + 1 = j + 1) = 0 :=
          hext (j + 1) (by omega) _
        obtain ⟨x₂, hx₂⟩ :=
          CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₃ κ hS e rfl he_ext
        -- `x₂ : Ext κ M j = 0` by `hext` at `j`.
        have hx₂_zero : x₂ = 0 := hext j (by omega) _
        rw [hx₂_zero] at hx₂
        simpa using hx₂.symm
      -- Step B: apply `ih (M := MxM)` at index `n`.
      have hdepth_MxM : (n : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) MxM :=
        (ih (M := MxM)).mpr hMxM_vanish
      -- Step C: unfold `depth MxM = sSup`-clause and extract a witness rs'
      --   of length ≥ n on MxM in 𝔪 (when n ≥ 1; the n = 0 case uses []).
      have hne_MxM : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) • (⊤ : Submodule R MxM) ≠ ⊤ :=
        Ne.symm (Submodule.top_ne_ideal_smul_of_le_jacobson_annihilator
          (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal_le_jacobson _))
      have hdepth_MxM_unfold :
          depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) MxM
            = sSup { k : ℕ∞ | ∃ rs : List R, (rs.length : ℕ∞) = k ∧
              (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)
              ∧ RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular MxM rs } := by
        rw [depth, if_neg hne_MxM]
      obtain ⟨rs', hrs'_len_ge, hrs'_mem, hrs'_reg⟩ :
          ∃ rs' : List R, n ≤ rs'.length ∧
            (∀ r ∈ rs', r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ∧
            RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular MxM rs' := by
        rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos n with hn0 | hn_pos
        · subst hn0
          exact ⟨[], by simp, by simp,
            RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular.nil R MxM⟩
        · have hlt : ((n - 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) <
              depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) MxM := by
            have h1 : ((n - 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) < (n : ℕ∞) := by
              exact_mod_cast Nat.sub_lt hn_pos Nat.one_pos
            exact h1.trans_le hdepth_MxM
          rw [hdepth_MxM_unfold] at hlt
          obtain ⟨k, ⟨rs', hrs'_len_eq, hrs'_mem, hrs'_reg⟩, hk_lt⟩ :=
            lt_sSup_iff.mp hlt
          have hrs'_len_gt : n - 1 < rs'.length := by
            rw [← hrs'_len_eq] at hk_lt
            exact_mod_cast hk_lt
          refine ⟨rs', ?_, hrs'_mem, hrs'_reg⟩
          omega
      -- Step D: truncate rs' to length n; resulting sequence is M-regular.
      let rs_n := rs'.take n
      have hrs_n_len : rs_n.length = n := by
        show (rs'.take n).length = n
        rw [List.length_take]
        omega
      have hrs_n_mem : ∀ r ∈ rs_n, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := fun r hr =>
        hrs'_mem r (List.mem_of_mem_take hr)
      have hrs_n_reg : RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular MxM rs_n := by
        show RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular MxM (rs'.take n)
        have hsplit : rs' = rs'.take n ++ rs'.drop n :=
          (List.take_append_drop _ _).symm
        have hwr : RingTheory.Sequence.IsWeaklyRegular MxM rs' :=
          hrs'_reg.toIsWeaklyRegular
        rw [hsplit] at hwr
        have hwr_n : RingTheory.Sequence.IsWeaklyRegular MxM (rs'.take n) :=
          ((RingTheory.Sequence.isWeaklyRegular_append_iff MxM _ _).mp hwr).1
        exact (IsLocalRing.isRegular_iff_isWeaklyRegular_of_subset_maximalIdeal
          hrs_n_mem).mpr hwr_n
      -- Step E: cons x to form a length-(n+1) M-regular sequence in 𝔪.
      have hrs_M_reg : RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular M (x :: rs_n) :=
        (RingTheory.Sequence.isRegular_cons_iff M x rs_n).mpr ⟨hxReg, hrs_n_reg⟩
      have hrs_M_mem : ∀ r ∈ (x :: rs_n), r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := by
        intro r hr
        rcases List.mem_cons.mp hr with rfl | hr
        · exact hxMem
        · exact hrs_n_mem r hr
      have hrs_M_len : (x :: rs_n).length = n + 1 := by simp [hrs_n_len]
      -- Step F: conclude `(n+1 : ℕ∞) ≤ depth M` via `le_sSup`.
      have hne_M : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) • (⊤ : Submodule R M) ≠ ⊤ :=
        Ne.symm (Submodule.top_ne_ideal_smul_of_le_jacobson_annihilator
          (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal_le_jacobson _))
      rw [depth, if_neg hne_M]
      apply le_sSup
      refine ⟨x :: rs_n, ?_, hrs_M_mem, hrs_M_reg⟩
      exact_mod_cast hrs_M_len

          depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M
            = sSup { k : ℕ∞ | ∃ rs : List R, (rs.length : ℕ∞) = k ∧
              (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)
              ∧ RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular M rs } := by
        rw [depth, if_neg hne_M]
      -- Step 2: extract a regular sequence rs of length > n in 𝔪 on M.
      have hlt : (n : ℕ∞) < depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M := by
        calc (n : ℕ∞)
            < ((n + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) := by exact_mod_cast Nat.lt_succ_self n
          _ ≤ _ := _hdepth
      rw [hdepth_M_unfold] at hlt
      obtain ⟨k, ⟨rs, hrs_len, hrs_mem, hrs_reg⟩, hk_lt⟩ := lt_sSup_iff.mp hlt
      have hrs_len_gt : n < rs.length := by
        rw [← hrs_len] at hk_lt
        exact_mod_cast hk_lt
      -- Step 3: rs is non-empty (length > n ≥ 0); decompose rs = x :: rs_tail.
      rcases rs with _ | ⟨x, rs_tail⟩
      · -- impossible: empty list has length 0, not > n.
        simp at hrs_len_gt
      have hxMem : x ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := hrs_mem x List.mem_cons_self
      have htail_mem : ∀ r ∈ rs_tail, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := fun r hr =>
        hrs_mem r (List.mem_cons_of_mem _ hr)
      have hcons := (RingTheory.Sequence.isRegular_cons_iff M x rs_tail).mp hrs_reg
      have hxReg : IsSMulRegular M x := hcons.1
      have hrs_tail_reg : RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular (QuotSMulTop x M) rs_tail :=
        hcons.2
      have htail_len_ge : n ≤ rs_tail.length := by
        have h1 : n < (x :: rs_tail).length := hrs_len_gt
        simp [List.length_cons] at h1
        omega
      -- Step 4: `x ∈ Ann(κ)` via `Ideal.annihilator_quotient`.
      have hannih : Module.annihilator R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R) =
          IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := Ideal.annihilator_quotient
      have hxAnnih : x ∈ Module.annihilator R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R) :=
        hannih ▸ hxMem
      -- Step 5: build MxM and show depth MxM ≥ n via the prefix rs_tail.take n.
      let MxM : Type u := QuotSMulTop x M
      haveI : Nontrivial MxM :=
        nontrivial_quotSMulTop_of_mem_maximalIdeal M hxMem
      haveI : _root_.Module.Finite R MxM := inferInstance
      have hne_MxM : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) • (⊤ : Submodule R MxM) ≠ ⊤ :=
        Ne.symm (Submodule.top_ne_ideal_smul_of_le_jacobson_annihilator
          (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal_le_jacobson _))
      let rs_n := rs_tail.take n
      have hrs_n_len : rs_n.length = n := by
        change (rs_tail.take n).length = n
        rw [List.length_take]
        omega
      have hrs_n_mem : ∀ r ∈ rs_n, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := fun r hr =>
        htail_mem r (List.mem_of_mem_take hr)
      have hrs_n_reg : RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular MxM rs_n := by
        change RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular MxM (rs_tail.take n)
        have hsplit : rs_tail = rs_tail.take n ++ rs_tail.drop n :=
          (List.take_append_drop _ _).symm
        have hwr : RingTheory.Sequence.IsWeaklyRegular MxM rs_tail :=
          hrs_tail_reg.toIsWeaklyRegular
        rw [hsplit] at hwr
        have hwr_n : RingTheory.Sequence.IsWeaklyRegular MxM (rs_tail.take n) :=
          ((RingTheory.Sequence.isWeaklyRegular_append_iff MxM _ _).mp hwr).1
        exact (IsLocalRing.isRegular_iff_isWeaklyRegular_of_subset_maximalIdeal
          hrs_n_mem).mpr hwr_n
      have hdepth_MxM : (n : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) MxM := by
        rw [depth, if_neg hne_MxM]
        apply le_sSup
        refine ⟨rs_n, ?_, hrs_n_mem, hrs_n_reg⟩
        exact_mod_cast hrs_n_len
      -- Step 6: apply ih (M := MxM) at index n.
      have hMxM_vanish : ∀ j < n, ∀ e : Abelian.Ext.{u}
          (ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R))
          (ModuleCat.of R MxM) j, e = 0 :=
        (ih (M := MxM)).mp hdepth_MxM
      -- Step 7: LES chase. Set up SES.
      let S : ShortComplex (ModuleCat.{u} R) :=
        ModuleCat.smulShortComplex (ModuleCat.of R M) x
      have hS : S.ShortExact := hxReg.smulShortComplex_shortExact
      set κ : ModuleCat.{u} R := ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R)
        with hκ
      -- `S.f = x • 𝟙_M` (definitional), hence `mk₀ S.f = x • mk₀ 𝟙`, hence
      -- `_e.comp (mk₀ S.f) (add_zero i) = x • _e = 0` (by helper at `x ∈ Ann κ`).
      have hSf_eq_smul : S.f = x • (𝟙 (ModuleCat.of R M) : _ ⟶ _) := rfl
      have hSf_kill :
          _e.comp (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀ S.f) (add_zero i) = 0 := by
        have hcomp :
            _e.comp (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀ S.f) (add_zero i) = x • _e := by
          rw [hSf_eq_smul, CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀_smul,
              CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.comp_smul,
              CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.comp_mk₀_id]
        rw [hcomp]
        exact ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator _e hxAnnih
      -- Split on i = 0 vs i ≥ 1.
      rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos i with hi0 | hi_pos
      · subst hi0
        -- Use mono S.f (since hxReg) + postcomp_mk₀_injective_of_mono.
        haveI hmono : CategoryTheory.Mono S.f := by
          rw [ModuleCat.mono_iff_injective]
          exact hxReg
        have hinj := CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.postcomp_mk₀_injective_of_mono κ S.f
        apply hinj
        simpa using hSf_kill
      · -- i ≥ 1: write i = j + 1, then j < n and use covariant_sequence_exact₁.
        obtain ⟨j, rfl⟩ := Nat.exists_eq_succ_of_ne_zero
          (Nat.pos_iff_ne_zero.mp hi_pos)
        have hj : j < n := by omega
        obtain ⟨x₃, hx₃⟩ :=
          CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₁ κ hS _e hSf_kill rfl
        have hx₃_zero : x₃ = 0 := hMxM_vanish j hj _
        rw [hx₃_zero] at hx₃
        simpa using hx₃.symm

800	
801	For a nonzero finite module `M` of finite projective dimension over a
802	Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)`, the **Auslander–Buchsbaum formula** reads
803	```
804	  pd_R(M) + depth(M) = depth(R)
805	```
806	(Stacks tag 090V). The proof inducts on `depth(M)`: the base case
807	`depth(M) = 0` uses a minimal finite free resolution of `M` and the
808	"what is exact" criterion (Stacks 00MF) plus iterated application of the
809	depth-on-a-short-exact-sequence lemma (§4) to bound `depth(R)` against the
810	resolution length; the inductive step picks a common non-zero-divisor
811	`x ∈ 𝔪` on both `R` and `M`, applies the snake lemma to obtain a minimal
812	finite free resolution of `M/xM` over `R/xR` of the same length, and uses
813	the inductive hypothesis on `M/xM` over `R/xR`.
814	
815	Blueprint reference: `thm:auslander_buchsbaum` (Stacks tag 090V). -/
816	
817	/-- **The Auslander–Buchsbaum formula.** Let `(R, 𝔪)` be a Noetherian local
818	ring and let `M` be a nonzero finite `R`-module of finite projective
819	dimension. Then
820	```
821	  pd_R(M) + depth_R(M) = depth(R).
822	```
823	
824	The hypothesis "finite projective dimension" is encoded by an explicit
825	upper bound `n : ℕ` on the projective dimension (so the formula compares
826	finite numeric quantities cleanly without `WithBot ℕ∞`-arithmetic
827	subtleties).
828

831	"what is exact" + iterated `Module.depth_of_short_exact`; inductive step
832	via the snake lemma on multiplication by a common non-zero-divisor and

834	file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/

292	    · exact ⟨n, fun i hi ↦ hasProjectiveDimensionLT_of_ge _ (n + 1) _ (by simpa using hi),

331	    le_antisymm_iff]
